title: 4% Paraformaldehye in .1M PB preparation
steps: Add 400 mL of MilliQ water to a 1000 mL, beaker Milli-Q® Integral Water Purification System for Ultrapure Water (filter, Millipore Sigma, ZRXQ010WW, http://www.emdmillipore.com/US/en/product/Milli-Q-Integral-10-Water-Purification-System,MM_NF-ZRXQ010WW )
Add stir bar in beaker and set on hot plate at 50 °C
Add 5 drops (500ul) of 10N NaOH (from 60ml dropper) Sodium Hydroxide, 10.0N Thomas Scientific Catalog #C871W32 (this makes the solution basic to help dissolve the paraformaldehyde)
Weigh 20 g of Paraformaldehyde and add to MilliQ water. (Stir about 01:00:00 or until all parafromaldehyde is dissolved and solution is clear) Paraformaldehyde Contributed by users Catalog #19200
Make 1 Molarity (M) Phosphate buffer at PH 7.4
Top off to 500ml with MilliQ water.
Filter solution through .2um CA member filter. Corning™ Disposable Vacuum Filter/Storage Systems Fisher Scientific Catalog #09-761-5
Lable container with '4% paraformaldehyed in .1M PB' and date. Store at 4 °C, for up to 1 week. (Can also be aliqouted into Falcon Tube (50 mL) Fischer Scientific and stored at -20 °C for 3 months.)
Prepare 1 Molarity (M) Soduim Phosphate Dibasic Dihydrate (Store stock at Room temperature .) Sodium Phosphate Dibasic Dihydrate (USP/FCC/EP/BP), Fisher Chemical Fisher Scientific Catalog #S472-500

Prepare 1 Molarity (M) Sodium Phospate Monobasic Anyhydrous (store stock at Room temperature ) Sodium Phosphate Monobasic Anhydrous (Colorless-to-White Crystals), Fisher BioReagent Fisher Scientific Catalog #BP329-500
To make 100 mL, 1M PB at PH 7.4 add 19 mL, Monobasic plus 81 mL, Dibasic (store as stock at Room temperature )
Add 50 mL, 1M PB PH 7.4 to paraformaldehyde solution.
Test PH and adjust solution to final desired pH using HCl or NaOH.
creator: Monique Copeland
